name: Amazon Lex V2
pricing_notes: Text requests $0.00075/request; voice $0.004/15s; Free: 10K text + 5K voice/mo first year

auth.notes: AWS IAM credentials (access key + secret or instance role); Cognito for end-user auth

performance.notes: Intent recognition ~150ms in us-east-1; regional latency varies; streaming dialog supported

agent_readiness.notes: AWS conversational AI service with intent/slot-based NLU. Lambda fulfillment hooks. Connect integration for contact centers. Streaming conversations. Multi-locale support. boto3 SDK for Python. Deep AWS integration (Connect, Kendra, Bedrock). Best when deeply embedded in AWS ecosystem.

gotchas: Intent/slot-based model limiting for complex conversations vs LLMs
IAM permissions complexity for multi-service integration
Lex V1 to V2 migration caused confusion — ensure using V2 APIs
Amazon Connect integration requires separate Contact Flows configuration
best_when: AWS-native contact center automation with Amazon Connect
Lambda-driven fulfillment in existing AWS architecture
Teams needing built-in Kendra search or Bedrock LLM integration
Regulated industries where AWS GovCloud deployment is required
avoid_when: Non-AWS environments (GCP Dialogflow, Azure CLU better aligned)
Open-ended conversational AI (modern LLMs more capable)
Teams without AWS expertise (steeper IAM/service learning curve)
summary: Amazon Lex V2 is AWS's conversational AI service with intent/slot-based NLU, Lambda fulfillment, and native Amazon Connect integration for contact centers. boto3 SDK. Deep AWS ecosystem integration (Kendra, Bedrock). Best for AWS-native organizations building contact center automation or voice bots that need to stay within the AWS ecosystem. Modern LLMs exceed it for open-ended dialog.
